content:
- Updated the task file to completed with passes=true and checked acceptance criteria, then ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully.
- Repo state is ready to publish: code/docs/tests plus `.ralph` bookkeeping are all in the worktree and all required lanes already passed.
- Next: `git add -A`, commit with a task-finished message that records the README/TDD changes and passing `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`, then `git push` and stop.

- Read the previous Choose-Task progress entry, refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, and reread the selection rules.
- Verified the active backlog contains five failing non-bug tasks, no active failing bugs, no visible blocker tags, and no visible priority tags; inspected the repo shape enough to confirm a Rust workspace with `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` lanes but no `.github/workflows` yet.
- Next I should set `.ralph/current_task.txt` to the CI pipeline foundation task `.ralph/tasks/story-15-ci-build-test-image-pipeline/01-task-build-master-only-pipeline-for-full-tests-and-scratch-ghcr-image.md` and quit immediately.

- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with `.ralph/tasks/story-15-ci-build-test-image-pipeline/01-task-build-master-only-pipeline-for-full-tests-and-scratch-ghcr-image.md`.
- Chose the CI pipeline foundation task because all active failing tasks are non-bugs with no visible blocker/priority overrides, and the actual repo is a Rust workspace with required `make` quality lanes but no GitHub workflow yet.
- Next agent should open that task, use the required TDD workflow, implement the master-only full-test plus scratch GHCR image pipeline, then validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all still pass.

- Read previous Ralph progress, `.ralph/current_task.txt`, the selected task file, and the `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the selected task is the master-only GHCR scratch-image CI pipeline task and the task file currently has no execution marker like `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn is heading toward creating a plan file rather than implementing.
- Next I should inspect the existing repo/testing/container boundaries, write the required plan under the task's `_plans` directory with explicit TDD and boundary-cleanup steps, mark it `TO BE VERIFIED`, record the plan path in the task file, and stop.

- Created the planning artifact `.ralph/tasks/story-15-ci-build-test-image-pipeline/01-task-build-master-only-pipeline-for-full-tests-and-scratch-ghcr-image_plans/2026-04-19-master-only-ghcr-scratch-pipeline-plan.md` and linked it from the selected task file.
- The plan is intentionally left at `TO BE VERIFIED`; it lays out TDD slices for workflow trigger checks, full-suite validation checks, GHCR commit-tag publish checks, scratch-image Dockerfile conversion, and a boundary cleanup that centralizes image/workflow contract ownership.
- Next turn should review that plan, switch it to `NOW EXECUTE` only if the design still looks right, and then stop immediately per the task instructions; no implementation or test execution was started in this planning pass.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and both required skill files (`tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries`).
- Confirmed the selected task is the master-only full-test plus GHCR scratch-image pipeline task, and there is already a plan artifact from the previous pass.
- Next I am reading the task file and linked plan to decide whether this turn must remain in verification mode or can switch the plan to `NOW EXECUTE` and stop immediately.

- Read the task file and the linked plan file; the task is still planning-gated because the plan ends with `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- Verified the core assumptions behind the plan still hold in the repo: no GitHub workflow exists yet, `Dockerfile` still ships a Debian runtime image, and the Docker/image runtime contract is split across multiple test-support files.
- Next I am doing one last repo scan for workflow/test/documentation touchpoints; if nothing contradicts the design, I should flip the plan marker to `NOW EXECUTE` and quit immediately without implementing.

- Re-read the selected task, the existing plan, the `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill guidance, and the current repository files the plan depends on.
- Verified the plan still matches the repo reality: there is no workflow yet, the Docker runtime image is still Debian-based, the required make lanes are `check`/`lint`/`test`/`test-long`, and the Docker/image contract is still split across multiple support helpers.
- I changed the plan terminator from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE` in `.ralph/tasks/story-15-ci-build-test-image-pipeline/01-task-build-master-only-pipeline-for-full-tests-and-scratch-ghcr-image_plans/2026-04-19-master-only-ghcr-scratch-pipeline-plan.md`. Next turn must execute that plan with strict TDD slices, keep boundary cleanup central, and stop to re-verify immediately if the design proves wrong.

- Read the active task file and linked execution plan.
- Verified the repo does not actually contain the planned implementation yet: no `.github/workflows/master-image.yml`, no `ci_contract.rs`, no `github_workflow_contract.rs`, and `Dockerfile` still uses `debian:bookworm-slim` for the runtime stage.
- Next I should execute the plan for real using vertical TDD slices, starting by reading the existing Docker/image test support and then creating the first failing workflow contract test.

- Added the first real CI contract test (`crates/runner/tests/ci_contract.rs`) and a typed YAML support parser (`crates/runner/tests/support/github_workflow_contract.rs`).
- Ran `cargo test -p runner --test ci_contract master_image_workflow_triggers_only_on_pushes_to_master`; it failed as expected because `.github/workflows/master-image.yml` does not exist yet.
- Next I should add the smallest workflow file that triggers only on push to master, rerun that test to green, and then continue with the next RED slice for validation-lane coverage.

- Executed the real TDD slices for the task: added workflow contract tests, brought in `.github/workflows/master-image.yml`, added publish and registry-boundary assertions, and converted `Dockerfile` to a scratch runtime image.
- The first scratch implementation hardcoded `x86_64-unknown-linux-musl`, and the ignored Docker long lane exposed that as wrong on this arm64 machine because the AWS-LC musl build failed; I refactored the Dockerfile to map `TARGETARCH` to the correct musl target and copy the built binary through a stable builder-owned path.
- I also used the final boundary pass to centralize image-entrypoint inspect args in `runner_docker_contract.rs` and replace silent teardown error swallowing in the Docker test harnesses with explicit presence checks plus real cleanup commands. Next I should run the required full lanes: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`.
